title: Lec-37: CSMA/CA in Computer Network | Full Explanation

segments.text: Dear students, welcome to Gate Smashers. In this video I am going to explain CSMA/CA
that is Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Avoidance
and in this we will discuss all important points related to CSMA/CA
which will help in your competitive exams, even in your college/University exams.
So guys quickly like the video, subscribe the channel if you haven't yet,
and if you have done then you can subscribe from more devices,
because subscribers are very important.
So let's start. If we talk about CSMA/CA, where do we use it?
Note this first point, we use it is wireless LAN,
you can call it wifi or you can call it IEEE802.11
So here we use what? CA, that is what?
Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Avoidance.
So Collision Avoidance is used in wifi, wireless.
Before this we talked about CSMA/CD. In that we talked about Collision Detection.
So Collision Detection in case of wired, Collision Avoidance in case of wireless.
First note this point. Now if I give you an overview,
I have explained everything about CSMA/CD.
So look, let's say if there is a station S, if S is transmitting a signal,
if at the same time it receives some other signal, then we call it Collision Detection.
Means at one point it is transmitting signal, one is this signal,
one already comes to it, means it already has one and one more extra comes to it,
means two signals came to it, then what happens there? Collision Detection.
In a way one energy it is already sending,
means the signal it sends, it's the sender's energy, let's say E1.
One more signal comes means in a way double energy signals come to it,
so it will detect from here that I am sending E1, but I get twice of E1,
because energy of both will be equal, so then it can detect that there is a collision.
But, what happens in case of wireless is,
when your data or signal is transmitted, when it travels it loses energy.
Due to this, it will not receive double energy signal.
Until it gets the double energy signal, it cannot detect that there is a collision.
That's why here we use Collision Avoidance. Note this first point,
so that wherever you are asked, you can easily write it.
Now I will tell you what is its strategy. I have made this whole flow chart.
So look, first let's say some station comes, it wants to start transmission,
so value of K is 0. Means its number of attempts at start, K means number of attempts,
let's say it comes first, so value of K at the start is 0.
First what it does? Sense. Because it is CSMA, it will first sense the carrier.
It senses, is it idle? If yes, then it will go down, if no, then it will go back up.
Going back up means it will sense again, it will sense again,
so when it gets idle, what it has to do? It has to wait.
Here never do data transfer directly, funda of waiting is most here...
So it will do what? Wait for IFS. What is the meaning of IFS? Inter Frame Space.
You can better call it DCIF or DIFS that is DCF Inter Frame Space,
and what is DCF? Distributed Coordination Function.
Note this small point, this is only used in case of wireless.
In a way what it does, it waits for some amount of time, what is the reason?
It is waiting because maybe some other station has already started transmission,
so maybe in future collision should not happen so in spite of being idle, because
where does it know it is idle? It knows only its own,
on its own, you can say, node it knows that yes there is idle.
You can say in the whole domain, it can't know if your whole domain is idle, no.
It can only see at its own end that yes, there is idle.
But it did not start immediately, it waited thinking that
maybe someone else may have started transmission before me. So it will wait.
After that comes Contention Window, choose a random number R between
0 to 2 to the power K minus 1. So let's say, if at the start, it's the first attempt.
Then value of this will be 0 to 2 raised to power 0 minus 1.
So 2 raised to power 0 is what? 1. So in a way it has to not wait.
Let's say, for example, if collision occurs
then after going round value of K will become 1 from 0.
Here value of K will become 1, let's say if collision occurs,
then what will be this value? 0 to 2 raised to power 1 minus 1.
Means again 0 to 1, means it will choose any of these two values.
After that 0 to, this value will become what after this? 2 raised to power 2,
means 0 to 3, then 0 to 7, means this value will keep growing exponentially,
and this slot means what? What will it do here? It will wait for a particular slot.
I mean to say, let's say I make fixed amount of slots,
let's say I make a slot of 5 seconds, so if it chooses value 0,
so 0 means it will not wait, it will immediately go to the next phase.
Let's say if it chooses value 1, means it will wait till 1 slot.
Let's say if it chooses value 3, then it will wait till 3 slots,
3 slots of 5 means how many? For 15 seconds it will wait then go to the next.
So this contention window means that there we are making it wait more.
Then comes, if let's say it chooses value 0, immediately it will try to transmit data,
then it will do what? Send RTS. RTS means Ready To Send signal it will send,
no data yet, this is just that I am ready to send. After doing ready to send,
again it puts a timer, it is called Short IFS, means less amount of time is there,
but it has to wait. Why it has to wait? Because it doesn't have a clear signal yet.
What I mean to say is it sends its signal that I am ready to send.
Ready to send signal will go where? Let's say you have a modem, in case of wifi,
let's say you have, call it a hub or modem,
and other devices are connected to it wirelessly.
So now what will happen is, ready to send will go to modem,
after that it has to send a Clear To Send signal. So ready to send is sent,
sender waits till timer, Clear To Send means if Clear To Send is received,
if Clear To Send is received before timeout,
before this timeout is over if it gets CTS signal, then what it has to do?
Again it has to wait. Why this wait? See, if modem already sends to it that
yes, you are Clear To Send. This sent Ready To Send, that sent Clear To Send.
Now after sending Clear To Send it is still waiting, its reason is
when Ready To Send comes, modem immediately stops all transmission,
that stop, already there is one who is Ready To Send. But it is possible that
just before this signal, someone else has started transmission,
due to this it will again wait, and then finally it will transmit the data.
So look, Send frame comes here so these many steps are to stop it, to make it wait.
And finally, it sends, if acknowledgement is recieved then success,
if acknowledgement is not recieved then there is collision.
And if acknowledgement is not recieved then again there is a timeout,
if acknowledgement is recieved before timeout then it's good, if it doesn't come,
then there is collision. So in case of collision again, value of K becomes 1.
What I mean to say is before it was its first attempt, now it is next attempt,
so if value of this attempt is less than the limit, till then it will go ahead.
If let's say the value becomes higher than the limit, abort.
Let's say you keep limit value 10, means no one can attempt more than 10 times,
it will be aborted, but if let's say value of K is less than the limit,
then till TB time, you can say that TB is the Backoff Time,
it will wait till TB time. And how do we calculate TB time?
Random number R which it chooses here, multiplied by Slot Time.
Slot time is generally equal to round trip time.
So we make it wait more saying wait, already you have made 1 attempt
but it has failed, so wait, now with K value 1, it will start the same process again.
So I will simply summarize this entire story for you.
We use Ready To Send, Clear To Send signals, but we make it wait again and again,
because there is no funda of detect here, we just have to avoid collision.
What is the best strategy to avoid? That you make the sender wait
so that immedately it can't send data, we make it wait.
That's why this type of strategy we use here.
So the whole story of CSMA/CA is just this.
More than this you don't have to study, this is more than sufficient.
Thank you.
